source_documents: Selmer-Maccaferri-D-hole-MM-01.pdf — Sheet 1/2: Body front view, bracing, bridge detail
Selmer-Maccaferri-D-hole-MM-02.pdf — Sheet 2/2: Neck plan, fret positions, side view, materials
metadata.source_title: Guitare jazz manouche 'dans le style' Maccaferri type 'D hole'
metadata.notes: Two-sheet complete plan. 'Dans le style' — in the style of Maccaferri, not exact reproduction. Fret positions extracted from sheet 2/2 fret table. Materials explicitly listed in English on sheet 2/2.

name: Selmer-Maccaferri D-hole
local_name: Guitare jazz manouche type D hole
description: Gypsy jazz guitar in the Selmer-Maccaferri tradition with distinctive D-shaped soundhole. The original Selmer-Maccaferri D-hole was produced 1932-1936. Arched soundboard (8mm arch across width at bridge). Floating walnut bridge with detachable middle section. Distinctive construction: ribs use maple with three Dural (aluminum alloy) internal plates perpendicular to sides. Neck is perpendicular to fingerboard — zero neck angle. Associated with Django Reinhardt and the Hot Club de France style.
cam_notes: Unique construction with arched top, floating bridge, and Dural rib plates. Requires specialized CAM approach distinct from flat-top acoustic generator.
classification_note: Although sometimes listed with flat-tops, the Selmer-Maccaferri is best classified separately. The soundboard has an 8mm arch across the bridge width. Construction is closer to a domed flat-top than a true carved archtop.

scale.scale_length_note: Derived from fret position table on Sheet 2/2. Fret 1 = 35.5mm → scale ≈ 632mm. Original Selmer D-hole used 670mm — this plan may be a modified version.

fret_positions_cumulative_mm.note: Cumulative distance from nut to each fret, read from Sheet 2/2 fret plan

soundhole.description: Distinctive D-shaped soundhole — rectangular with one rounded side. Positioned in upper bout. This shape is the defining visual feature of the pre-1936 Selmer guitars.
soundhole.source: Sheet 1/2 upper left D-hole detail drawing

top_arch.arch_description: Soundboard arched 8mm across width at bridge
top_arch.arch_source: Sheet 1/2 annotation: Soundboard arched 8mm across width at bridge
top_arch.arch_notes: Not carved like a true archtop. The soundboard halves are bent and then trimmed and joined at center seam.

body.bracing_description: Transverse ladder-style braces on top. Back braces also transverse. Construction note: soundboard halves are bent at center and then trimmed and joined.
body.bracing_construction_note: Soundboard halves are bent at this point and then trimmed and joined — Sheet 2/2
body.bridge_position_note: Bridge not glued to top — floating bridge held by string tension only. Standard for arched-top and gypsy jazz guitars.

neck.neck_width_source: 47mm — Sheet 2/2 fret plan width at nut
neck.neck_angle_note: Neck perpendicular to fingerboard — zero neck angle. Stated in materials list: NECK: PERPENDICULAR TO FINGERBOARD
neck.joint_source: Dovetail joint visible in Sheet 2/2 neck section view

bridge.source: BRIDGE: WALNUT WITH EBONY TOP (HOLLOWED OUT), SIDE PIECES GLUED TO SOUNDBOARD, MIDDLE DETACHABLE — Sheet 2/2 materials

materials.back_note: Laminated construction — rosewood outer, mahogany inner
materials.ribs_note: Maple sides with three Dural (aluminum alloy) plates — distinctive Maccaferri construction. The Dural plates are internal stiffeners running perpendicular to the side thickness.
materials.neck_note: Neck set perpendicular to fingerboard — zero neck angle

distinctive_construction_features: D-shaped soundhole — not round, not oval
Arched soundboard 8mm at bridge — bent not carved
Floating detachable bridge — middle section removable
Ribs with three Dural (aluminum alloy) internal plates — unique to Maccaferri tradition
Zero neck angle — neck perpendicular to fingerboard
Soundboard construction: halves bent, trimmed, and joined at center seam
Side pieces of bridge glued to top, middle section detachable for adjustment

historical_context.original_instrument: Selmer guitar designed by Mario Maccaferri, produced 1932-1952
historical_context.association: Django Reinhardt and Hot Club de France
historical_context.this_plan: In the style of the original — not an exact reproduction

variants.name: D-hole Style
variants.description: Full-size plan in the Maccaferri D-hole tradition. Spruce top, laminated rosewood/mahogany back, maple ribs with Dural plates, ebony fingerboard, floating walnut/ebony bridge.
pending: Body outline tracing in Fusion 360 — body front view on Sheet 1/2
Lower bout and upper bout width measurements
